heroHeadline: Artemis III Core Stage Enters KSC VAB; Orion Returns Home; Senate CJS Bipartisan Rejection of NASA Budget Cut
heroSubtitle: Apr 28-29: Day 28-29 post-splashdown brought a pair of major hardware milestones at Kennedy Space Center. On April 28, the Artemis III SLS core stage — 212 feet of liquid hydrogen tank, liquid oxygen tank, intertank, and forward skirt — was offloaded from the Pegasus barge and moved inside the Vehicle Assembly Building's transfer aisle, beginning integration preparations for High Bay 2. Simultaneously, the Artemis II Orion crew module arrived at KSC's Multi-Payload Processing Facility after its cross-country journey from San Diego, where technicians began de-servicing: extracting avionics for reuse, removing experiment packages, and offloading residual propellants. The 30-day formal heat shield inspection (Marshall X-ray scans + Avcoat sample extractions) is nearing completion; results expected ~May 10 will define the Artemis IV 2028 crewed lunar landing timeline. On the congressional front, the Senate CJS Appropriations Subcommittee (Chair Jerry Moran R-KS, Ranking Member Chris Van Hollen D-MD) held its FY2027 hearing on April 28 — both parties congratulated Isaacman on Artemis II's success and then jointly rejected the $18.8B budget request as inadequate, mirroring bipartisan House opposition from April 22-27. Isaacman revealed the Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope may launch August 2026 (ahead of September target) and committed to the Space Reactor-1 Freedom nuclear fission Mars mission in 2028. By April 29, VAB crane crews conducted rigging inspections for the Artemis III vertical lift while Artemis II de-servicing continued at the MPPF, and the crew completed week five of physiological reconditioning at JSC.
footerNote: Data sourced from NASA.gov, OIG Reports, SpaceNews, Ars Technica, Congressional appropriations. Tier 1: NASA/ESA/Official. Tier 2: SpaceNews/Ars Technica/Reuters. Tier 3: Planetary Society/AIAA.
